response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced web and backend software developer with a strong track record in full-stack engineering, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but has no discernible alignment with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, SIMD/MIMD architectures, and deep-learning workload analysis — none of which appear anywhere in the resume. His AI exposure is limited to agentic workflow tooling at the application layer, which is categorically different from hardware-level AI accelerator optimization. The candidate's education (BS in Computer Science) combined with roughly 13 years of relevant experience meets the minimum threshold on paper, but the domain mismatch is fundamental and not bridgeable through on-the-job ramp-up at a principal level.
strengths: Over a decade of professional software engineering experience across diverse domains including embedded systems, low-latency services, and cloud infrastructure
Demonstrated performance engineering mindset with async I/O, caching, and high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Exposure to AI-adjacent developer tooling and agentic infrastructure at re:cinq
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or any layer of the silicon stack
No background in deep learning workloads, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator optimization — the core technical requirements of this role
Currently in a career gap and most recent relevant engineering role was senior IC level, well below the principal/staff hardware architect seniority this position demands
